description: Trigger discrimination for RENDERING non-happy-path states (this skill) vs the error MODEL behind them (error-taxonomy-designer), vs mobile viewport/touch craft (mobile-viewport-craft), vs VERIFYING state accessibility (accessibility-test-harness).

cases.prompt: Design the empty, loading, and error states for this screen — including the first-run vs filtered-empty difference, skeletons, and what shows when a fetch fails.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — empties, loading visuals, and error presentation are UI state design. No error codes/retryable model and no breakpoint/touch craft are being designed.
cases.reason: The per-view state matrix (empty/loading/error/partial/offline) is this skill's core deliverable, distinct from the error model and from responsive craft.

cases.prompt: Define our error categories and stable machine codes, which errors are retryable, the envelope shape, and a rule that messages never leak internals.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse, within pack) — a code taxonomy, retryability, envelope, and disclosure rule are the error model. Rendering it is downstream.
cases.reason: error-taxonomy-designer owns the codes/messages/retryability. This skill consumes them to render error states.

cases.prompt: On phones our layout overflows, tap targets are tiny, and 100vh hides content behind the browser bar. Fix the responsive/touch behavior.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — breakpoints, touch-target sizing, and viewport-unit handling are mobile craft, not empty/loading/error state design.
cases.reason: mobile-viewport-craft owns responsive/touch/viewport correctness. This skill designs the states a view can be in, independent of screen size.

cases.prompt: Set up a test pass that verifies our loading and error states are announced to screen readers and keyboard-operable, with axe scans in CI.
cases.reason: Designing the verification harness (axe scans, keyboard/screen-reader checks, CI wiring) is accessibility-test-harness. This skill designs the states and records a11y intent; it does not run the verification.
